sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Nausea and vomiting are common symptoms with many possible causes, ranging from self-limited gastroenteritis to bowel obstruction, pregnancy-related illness, toxic exposure, or serious intracranial and cardiac disease.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Hypersalivation, retching, clamminess, sweating, pallor, or abdominal discomfort
- Associated headache, dizziness, fever, diarrhoea, or abdominal pain
- Red flags include intractable vomiting, haematemesis, altered conscious state, bilious or faeculent vomit, and significant dehydration

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Ondansetron can prolong the QT interval; use caution in known or suspected long-QT syndrome or other significant QT-risk settings
- Use of ondansetron in early pregnancy should follow local policy and clinician judgement; dehydration and inability to tolerate fluids are higher prehospital priorities
- Pregnant patients with uncontrolled hyperemesis are not suitable to remain on site
- Nausea and vomiting may be medication related, including GLP-1 agonists such as semaglutide or tirzepatide
- If the cause cannot be determined, consider serious underlying disease

sections.title: Management – Primary
sections.content:
- Primary Survey
- Provide reassurance
- Complete Vital Sign Survey, including blood glucose where appropriate
- Consider likely cause and appropriate antiemetic if within scope; otherwise consider non-pharmacological relief such as isopropyl alcohol inhalation where appropriate
- Consider urinalysis if reduced fluid intake or dehydration is suspected
- Consider oral rehydration if tolerated
- Consider analgesia where appropriate and tolerated
- Secondary survey where appropriate
- Consult the on-call doctor if IV fluids are required and not within scope

sections.title: Management – Advanced
sections.content:
- Consider IV access
- Consider IV fluids where indicated, guided by dehydration severity and comorbidity
- Consider ECG where indicated
- Consider point-of-care bloods where appropriate
- Consider other causes and treat accordingly
- Transport for further review and management if any red flags are evident

sections.title: Additional Information
sections.content:
- Ondansetron is less effective for vestibular causes of nausea and vomiting
- Isopropyl alcohol swab inhalation may provide short-term nausea relief in some patients
- Undifferentiated nausea and vomiting may reflect ACS, opioid effects, intracranial disease, severe gastroenteritis, or bowel obstruction
